active_themes.label: Confirmation Under Duress
active_themes.summary: Warsh confirmation proceeding as expected, but the frame has shifted from leadership transition to capitulation signal. If confirmed while Powell simultaneously faces Trump-adjacent pressure and the Iran crisis deepens, markets will interpret the outcome as evidence that Fed independence is conditional—not absolute. This is not a personnel change; it is a legitimacy test.

active_themes.label: Iran Escalation Test
active_themes.summary: Iran seized a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz today, the most direct challenge to US naval authority since ceasefire extension was announced. Oil traders have not repriced blockade duration risk—WTI remains below $95—suggesting market confidence in negotiated resolution remains intact. This is the decisive test: does Iran's escalation force markets to abandon the 3-week assumption and reprice to 3-6 months, or does it remain theatrical pressure within negotiation bounds?

active_themes.label: Decisions Made Under Threat
active_themes.summary: Pattern accelerated: Navy Secretary Phelan fired, SPLC under DOJ investigation, Apple cutting savings rates, student loan programs collapsing, Sullivan & Cromwell apologizing for AI errors in filings. None are scandals in isolation. Collectively they show decisions increasingly made for political rather than economic reasons—at precisely the moment when policy credibility is most needed. Markets have not priced the cost of this operating model yet.

active_themes.label: Credit Access Narrowing
active_themes.summary: Apple Card savings rate cut (15 basis points) and collapse of federal Grad PLUS loan access are isolated decisions but same underlying cause: lenders reducing exposure and rates. Student loan borrowers are forced into private alternatives (College Ave), which carry higher cost and shorter term flexibility. This is not a demand shock yet, but it is the setup for one if employment weakens.

open_watchlist.condition: Iran announces official negotiating delegation within 48 hours
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms oil traders' bet that talks restart before supply destruction becomes irreversible; keeps oil below $95 and reduces tail risk of 3-6 month blockade.

open_watchlist.condition: WTI oil closes above $95 for two consecutive trading sessions
open_watchlist.meaning: Signals market repricing of Iran supply crisis duration or severity; forces simultaneous reassessment of Fed independence risk if Warsh confirms at same time oil is spiking.

open_watchlist.condition: Fed official (Powell, governor, or staff) makes public statement on rates that sounds politically motivated rather than data-driven
open_watchlist.meaning: First explicit signal that institutional capture has moved from theory to practice; would likely trigger equities sell-off and 10-year yield spike.

open_watchlist.condition: Senate Democrats signal willingness to vote against Warsh confirmation (not delay, but actual opposition votes above 10 senators)
open_watchlist.meaning: Would slow timeline and force Warsh to make explicit commitments on Fed independence; could delay confirmation into June and create window where market reprices the regime shift risk.

open_watchlist.condition: Iran announces official negotiating delegation with specific names and mandate statement within 48 hours
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms talks are moving from ceasefire extension to actual negotiation; opens door to 3-6 week resolution timeline instead of indefinite blockade.

open_watchlist.condition: WTI oil closes above $100 for two consecutive trading days
open_watchlist.meaning: Signals market no longer believes Iran talks will hold; reprices blockade duration from temporary to structural; forces stagflation repricing in equities.

open_watchlist.condition: Powell makes public statement acknowledging Trump's May timeline or hinting at stepping down
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms Fed leadership is being rewritten under political pressure during active crisis; market reprices institutional credibility and Fed independence premium disappears.

open_watchlist.condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises above 4.2% while S&P 500 stays flat or declines
open_watchlist.meaning: Signals market is pricing inflation or institutional risk premium (not growth), indicating dual crisis is being repriced as stagflation rather than contained geopolitical shock.

open_watchlist.condition: Apple Card savings account or other non-bank lender cuts rates again within one week
open_watchlist.meaning: Credit tightening is broadening beyond Apple; signals beginning of supply-side credit crunch affecting consumer spending within 6–8 weeks.

open_watchlist.condition: Fed official makes public statement on rates that references Trump, politics, or May timeline
open_watchlist.meaning: Confirms that Fed independence is now conditional on political acceptance; investors begin reducing duration exposure and repricing long-end yields on credibility discount.
